mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the t-statistic for the return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with a rank correlation above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of cases during early-cycle phases across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently coincide with positive forward returns or fail to show a statistically significant negative return differential.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations or shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity risk premium compression, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side disruption and earnings downgrade risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production months fail to predict negative forward returns with a win rate below 40% in walk-forward testing.
